# Default Pick and Place Logic

## Overview

In the demo project, the supervision interface runs the same pick and place logic for each robot:

* New events are evaluated. Events are generated by the robots when specific conditions occur (for example, pick done, place done, pick target lost, etc.)
* Based on the received events, the status of the robot gripper is updated. For example, if a pick action was done, the information of the picked target is stored in the gripper structure stTaskInterface.stStatus.stGripper and the target is removed from the targets handler.
* Based on the phase of the task (either pick or place) and the status of the robot gripper (for example, empty, full, or some intermediate filling level), the logic determines if the phase is completed or if it should continue. For example, if the robot is in pick phase and the gripper is full, it moves to the place phase.
* During the task phase, the logic evaluates if a robot is waiting for a command or if it is busy. If the robot is waiting then the supervisor interface searches for a valid target for each listed system in either G\_astRoboticCellPickSearchLogic or G\_astRoboticCellPlaceSearchLogic (depending on the task phase) in the array aetSystemToSearch.
* If a valid target is found, the robot moves to process the selected target by creating a new command with the interface stTaskInterface.stCommand.
* If no valid target is found, the robot moves to a wait position described with the parameters G\_astRoboticCellPickSearchLogic or G\_astRoboticCellPlaceSearchLogic in the structure variable stSystemToWait.

## Example Code

To configure the system so that the supervisor interface should:

* Search for a pick target for Robot1 on the system Tracking1.
* If a target is not found on Tracking1, search for a pick target on the system Tracking3.
* If a target is not found on Tracking3, move to a wait position on Tracking1.

The logic can be configured as follows:

```
//select Robot1
etRobotId := SERT.ET_SystemEntity.Robot1;
//list of systems to search when looking for a pick target
G_astRoboticCellPickSearchLogic[etRobotId].aetSystemToSearch[1] := ROB.ET_CoordinateSystem.Tracking1;
G_astRoboticCellPickSearchLogic[etRobotId].aetSystemToSearch[2] := ROB.ET_CoordinateSystem.Tracking3;
G_astRoboticCellPickSearchLogic[etRobotId].udiNumberOfSystemsToSearch := 2;
G_astRoboticCellPickSearchLogic[etRobotId].etSystemToWait := ROB.ET_CoordinateSystem.Tracking1;
```
